creator: ard_1@mail.ru
text:
(In reply to Palle Girgensohn from comment #27)
And one more thing about the graylog plugin - fluentd documentation states that v.0.12 plugin API is backward compatible with fluentd v.1.x ( https://docs.fluentd.org/plugin-development )so I guess that if we can patch that dependency line somehow in the gem file, then there are some chances that this plugin can work with the latest fluentd version. But I don't know how to easily do this in the current port infrastructure. Moreover since I don't use this plugin in production I currently doesn't know how to test it functionality after such patching.
So the easiest thing will be to deprecate the graylog fluentd plugin due to incompatibility  with the latest versions of the fluentd in the plugin's gem configuration file.
Another option will be to switch other to the some actively developed graylog (GELF) plugin, but I can't recommend one since I don't use it.
